name: OpenHands
description: Open-source autonomous coding agent platform (formerly OpenDevin) from All Hands AI, distributed as CLI, SDK, local GUI, and cloud.

description.value: Open-source autonomous coding agent platform (formerly OpenDevin) that plans, edits, and executes code inside a sandboxed runtime; distributed as CLI, Python SDK, local GUI, and cloud.

creator.comment: Founded by Robert Brennan, Xingyao Wang, and Graham Neubig; project originated from the OpenDevin community (Binyuan Hui, Junyang Lin) and was rebranded under All Hands AI in late 2024.

license.comment: Core openhands and agent-server are MIT; the enterprise/ directory ships under a separate commercial license.

pricing-model.comment: Free OSS self-hosted tier, free Individual SaaS tier (10 conversations/day), paid Enterprise tier with SaaS or VPC self-hosted options.

covered-phases.comment: Planning Mode (beta) produces an explicit plan that the user approves before code changes; the agent then executes. The workflow does not prescribe distinct spec, review, or deployment phases.

methodology-style.comment: OpenHands is primarily an agent + runtime platform; the workflow value is in the harness (sandbox, event stream, CLI/SDK/GUI), not a prescriptive spec-driven methodology.

opinionated.comment: The agent is free-form; there is no enforced phase ordering or mandatory artifact set.

ceremony-level.comment: No formal review gates, ADRs, or multi-artifact ceremony required; planning is optional.

mechanization-level.comment: End-to-end managed pipeline: one command launches an agent that plans, edits, runs tests, and opens PRs against a repo/issue.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Default CLI confirmation mode asks for confirmation on actions; --always-approve disables it.

approval-gates.comment: Per-action confirmation on implementation steps in default mode. Planning Mode adds an optional plan-approval gate when enabled.

autonomous-e2e.comment: With --always-approve (or --llm-approve LLM-based security analyzer), the agent runs from issue to PR without human intervention.

autonomy-level.comment: Default configuration asks for confirmation on individual actions; rated against default, not the opt-in autonomous mode.

rollback-support.comment: Isolation is provided via Docker sandboxing, but there is no first-class artifact/code rollback command in the workflow; reverts rely on git.

supported-agents.comment: OpenHands is its own agent runtime rather than a workflow wrapped around Claude Code / Cursor / etc.; any LLM (Claude, GPT, MiniMax, etc.) can power it.

editor-integrations.comment: VS Code extension is built and released from the repo; CLI for terminal-only use; Local GUI and Cloud are web-based. No official JetBrains plugin found.

model-agnostic.comment: Works with Claude, GPT, MiniMax, and any LLM; users bring their own key for self-hosted.

mcp-support.comment: First-party MCP client across CLI, SDK, Local GUI, and Cloud; MCP servers configurable via ~/.openhands/mcp.json.

artifact-formats.comment: Context files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) and skills are markdown; the agent's output is primarily code changes.

artifacts-covered.comment: Planning Mode produces a plan artifact. No first-class spec, ADR, roadmap, or release-notes generation in the default workflow.

project-layout.value: .agents/skills/ (current) or .openhands/skills/ / .openhands/microagents/ (deprecated); root-level AGENTS.md (or CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md) for always-on context.

artifacts-in-git.comment: Skills and context files live inside the repository alongside source code.

language-agnostic.comment: Agent interacts via shell, Python/Jupyter, and browser; not tied to any specific programming language.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Skills/AGENTS.md inject instructional context; the default confirmation mode provides manual approval on each action. No structural hooks or CI gates ship as part of the workflow itself.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Agent loop is free-form; there is no mechanism that refuses to advance out of order.

artifact-validation.comment: Skills and context files are free-form markdown; no schema validation beyond optional frontmatter for keyword triggers.

memory-implementation.comment: Persistent context lives in markdown skill files and AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, loaded into the system prompt at conversation start.

persistent-memory.comment: Skills and AGENTS.md persist across sessions via files in the repo/home; CLI --resume also reloads prior conversations.

project-context-files.comment: Automatically loads AGENTS.md (and CLAUDE.md / GEMINI.md variants) from the project root.

cost-tracking.comment: Token/cost accounting is surfaced per conversation/run in the CLI and Cloud UI; no explicit budget-limit halt feature documented for the OSS workflow.

run-history.comment: Conversations are stored; --resume can list recent sessions and replay the most recent. Event-stream architecture records typed events.

telemetry-opt.comment: Could not find a clear disclosed telemetry opt-in/opt-out policy in the documentation reviewed; self-hosted deployments appear to run locally without mandatory telemetry but no explicit toggle was documented.

custom-templates.comment: Users author their own skills (markdown + optional frontmatter) at repo and user scope.

hooks.comment: No user-facing pre/post-phase hook API documented; customization flows through skills and SDK code, not lifecycle hooks.

sub-agents.comment: Skills (formerly microagents) are user-defined roles/behaviors that the agent invokes; SDK also supports delegating to sub-agents.

marketplace.comment: Official global skill registry at github.com/OpenHands/extensions for community-shared skills.

install-mechanism.comment: Recommended: `uv tool install openhands --python 3.12` (Python/pip ecosystem). Also: curl install.sh shell script, Docker image (binary), and a VS Code extension.

setup-time.comment: First run requires Python 3.12 + uv (or Docker), plus an interactive LLM setup saved to ~/.openhands/settings.json; comfortably under 30 minutes on a prepared workstation.

self-hosted.comment: OSS tier runs fully locally via CLI, GUI, or SDK with user-supplied LLM key.

requires-account.comment: No account is needed for self-hosted OSS; OpenHands Cloud is optional and requires a GitHub/GitLab login.

git-worktree-support.comment: Parallel/isolated workstreams use Docker sandboxes rather than git worktrees; no first-party worktree feature documented.

documentation-quality.comment: Broad coverage across CLI, SDK, GUI, Cloud, skills, and MCP with code examples; some pages (e.g. planning mode, hooks, telemetry) are thin or missing.

github-stars.comment: 71.6k stars as shown on the repo page on 2026-04-21.

active-maintenance.comment: Version 1.6.0 released 2026-03-30, well within 90 days of 2026-04-21.

example-projects.comment: Skill registry repo provides example skills; published paper includes benchmark case studies.

community-channel.comment: Primary community channel is Slack (invite link at dub.sh/openhands); also @OpenHandsDev on X.
